note: Located in [¶3] discussing mootness. This is part of a quoted legal principle and accuracy is critical.

evidence: PDF source line 63 shows 'In re' followed by a line break, then 'Guardianship/Conservatorship'. The word 'make' appears to be an OCR artifact injected between 'In re' and 'Guardianship'. The citation 'In re Guardianship/Conservatorship of Van Sickle' is a proper case name that should not contain 'make'.
note: Located in [¶3], part of the citation to 2005 ND 69. The erroneous 'make' corrupts the case name.

old_exact: [¶8] in relevant part: A parent, adult family or household member, guardian, or other custodian of any child, who willfully commits any of the following offenses is guilty of a class C felony: 1. Fails to provide proper parental care or control, subsistence, education as required by law, or other care or control necessary for the child's physical, mental, or emotional health, or morals. N.D.C.C. § 14-09-22.1 (emphasis added).
new_exact: [¶8] Section 14-09-22.1, N.D.C.C. provides the offense of "neglect of child." It provides, in relevant part: A parent, adult family or household member, guardian, or other custodian of any child, who willfully commits any of the following offenses is guilty of a class C felony: 1. Fails to provide proper parental care or control, subsistence, education as required by law, or other care or control necessary for the child's physical, mental, or emotional health, or morals. N.D.C.C. § 14-09-22.1 (emphasis added).
evidence: The database text begins [¶8] with 'in relevant part:' but the authoritative PDF shows [¶8] should begin with 'Section 14-09-22.1, N.D.C.C. provides the offense of "neglect of child." It provides, in relevant part:'. This opening sentence is currently located at the end of [¶7], indicating an ingestion defect where a paragraph boundary was lost.

In the PDF source (pdftotext -layout), the section heading 'III' appears on line 243, positioned between the end of [¶21] (line 241) and the beginning of [¶22] (line 245). The DB incorrectly places 'III' after [¶22]'s conclusion and before [¶23]. This is a section header placement error: III should precede [¶22], not follow it.

The PDF shows 'III' as a centered section header appearing after the conclusion of [¶21] and before the start of [¶22]. This proposal inserts 'III' into its correct structural position. Together with Proposal 1, this moves the section header from its current incorrect position (after [¶22]) to its correct position (before [¶22]).

evidence: The PDF pdftotext extraction shows 'intrudes\non the' at lines 201-202. The preposition 'on' is grammatically correct with 'intrudes' (one intrudes on something), whereas 'in' is incorrect.
